# Transcrição: 6. Social Media as Follow-Up

- **Fonte:** 6. Social Media as Follow-Up
- **Duração:** 01:17
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:17]

Social media's follow up. Look Twitter, Facebook, LinkedIn are ways to follow people up. Twitter is a great way actually to stay in touch with your customer. To follow up and stay on top of mind. Out of obscurity. Remember obscurity is your big problem. I forget you or I don't know you. Target search with Twitter. You basically just go up into the Target search bar, put in whatever you want. Plumbing companies, people searching plumbing. Maybe plumbing just in Nashville, plumbing in the United States, whatever. You can literally confine your searches that tight. Now when using Twitter, as you might know, I use Twitter quite effectively. Always, always have a photo in your avatar. Okay, it is ridiculous that you wouldn't have an avatar with a photo. In fact, I won't follow you if you don't have a photo in your avatar because I don't follow lazy people and I don't follow stupid people. Put your photo in there. And the photo should not be of your dog. Okay, or your baby. Those are nice things and it shouldn't be of your family. It should be of your face. Okay, straight on shot. Lights in your face. Not a sad shot. Straight on shot. Always include a photo of your avatar. Look, Twitter, if you don't get it now, Twitter is a great way to follow up your customers. Be creative with it. Be there. Have an avatar. Use it and use it every day.
